observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the lead escalation with detailed analysis and three resolution options — exactly what the rubric demands.
Clock-start ambiguity given its own dedicated escalation with clear options (order placement vs pickup) and a recommendation.
Wrong-address exclusion captured as BR-6 (invariant: true) with correct justification, plus a dedicated escalation for the adjudication process gap.
Auto-refund (BR-7, 10-minute trigger) vs 3-business-day processing (BR-8) tension is present, and the edge case about driver cancellation at minute 110 exposes the interaction between these rules.
Eight business rules extracted — exceeds the minimum five — with thoughtful invariant classifications and testable assertions for each.
Five formal escalations covering contradiction, ambiguity, and missing context — exceeds the minimum two.

Rejection reasons explicitly state why acceptance criteria cannot be written, with direct references to affected business rules — strong domain authority stance.
Nine edge cases identified, many beyond what the rubric expected (multiple driver cancellations, inventory gaps, address change post-order, boundary conditions).
Acceptance criteria intentionally left empty with a principled explanation — Bird refuses to write criteria against a contradictory spec, which is the correct domain-authority call.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met. PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated as the first and most prominent escalation with three resolution options. Clock-start ambiguity identified in confidence.assumptions ('The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time — PM said within 2 hours and Operations referenced pickup as a sub-step'). Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-6 and surfaced again in edge case conflict with BR-7. Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict partially addressed in business_impact.financial ('3-business-day refund processing window') and the BR-7 auto-refund mechanism, though the direct tension between immediate auto-triggering and 3-day processing is not called out as a formal contradiction. 8 rules extracted (exceeds minimum of 5) with reasonable invariant classifications and strong justifications. 3 formal escalations (exceeds minimum of 2). Confidence level of 38 is well below the 50 threshold. The output also goes significantly beyond minimum requirements with detailed edge cases, stakeholder impact analysis, and a rejection_reasons section.

observations.text: PM/Legal contradiction is the centerpiece of the analysis — identified as 'mutually exclusive product definitions' with three concrete resolution options and a recommendation for a hybrid approach. Exactly the kind of escalation expected.
Excellent edge case coverage (10 scenarios) including the BR-6/BR-7 conflict, boundary conditions, cascading cancellations, and stock-out gaps that weren't in the source material.
Confidence level of 38 with well-reasoned separation of high-confidence and low-confidence areas demonstrates appropriate uncertainty given the contradictions.
Ubiquitous language section reframes 'guaranteed delivery' as 'Goodwill Refund' — showing Bird internalized Legal's position rather than blindly adopting PM's framing.
Rejection reasons section explicitly blocks acceptance criteria creation until the guarantee-vs-best-effort contradiction is resolved — strong domain authority stance.

Third escalation correctly identifies five missing-context items (driver compensation, no-driver availability, stock-outs, distance calculation method, wrong-address adjudication) and recommends blocking implementation.

Clock-start ambiguity is noted as an assumption rather than escalated as a formal ambiguity. The expected behavior calls out that if pickup takes 14 minutes, the customer has only 1h46m of effective delivery time — this operational consequence is not surfaced.

Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict is only tangentially addressed in business_impact.financial. The direct tension — BR-7 triggers an immediate auto-refund but Finance says refunds take 3 business days to process — is not called out as a contradiction or escalation.
BR-2 (pickup SLA) and BR-3 (driver rating) classified as invariant: false where the rubric expected invariant: true. The justifications are reasonable but diverge from expected classifications.
